content.content:
1	# Orpheus web app redesign — design spec
2	
3	- **Date:** 2026-04-17
4	- **Branch:** `feat/web-redesign`
5	- **Scope:** `apps/web/**`, plus targeted extensions in `orpheus-core`, `orpheus-server`, `orpheus-tauri/src-tauri` to support the new UI.
6	- **Out of scope:** extractor logic, crypto code, CLI (except kept in sync per project rules).
7	
8	## 1. Goals
9	
10	1. **Fix broken CSS.** Every component references `--color-bronze`, `--color-verdigris`, `--color-rust`, `--color-fg`, `--color-fg-dim`, `--color-fg-faint`, `--color-rule`, `--color-bg-inset`, `--color-bronze-lit`. **None are defined** — `apps/web/src/index.css` contains only unrelated starter tokens (`--text`, `--bg`, `--border`, `--accent`). The app renders on browser defaults. This is the first thing to fix.
11	2. **Strip the Greek-mythology theming.** The name **Orpheus** stays. Everything else goes: Ω glyph, `καταβαίνω`, "to descend", "Descent" navigation, Roman numerals (I / II / III / IV) as panel numbers, "Cast the phrase", "Begin descent", "silence in the hall", "Retrieved from the underworld", "a warning from the guide", serif display type, bronze/verdigris/rust palette, `grain` overlay, `text-shadow` glow halos, `panel-enter`/`glyph-enter`/`descent-marker` animations.
12	3. **Turn the app into a navigable explorer.** Today: "form → static result list." Target: bookmarkable routes for results / wallet / address / import-instructions with clear CTAs that end on *"I have my key and I know how to use it."*
13	
14	## 2. Non-goals
15	
16	- No changes to extractor logic, crypto routines, or `orpheus-core::balance::VALID_PROVIDERS` membership.
17	- No Tauri shell redesign beyond the new `#[tauri::command]` endpoints needed for folder-picking and transaction lookups.
18	- No keyboard shortcut palette (⌘K).
19	- No sweep/sign/broadcast in-app. Orpheus extracts and explains. It does not transact. That boundary is non-negotiable.
20	- No fiat price fetching. Render a USD estimate only when the balance provider response happens to include a price; otherwise omit. No new price feed.
21	- No server-side persistence of scan results. Session-scoped only.
22	
23	## 3. Visual system
24	
25	### Style
26	
27	Linear/Vercel-adjacent: flat, dark-default, sans-serif, one accent color, generous-but-not-wasteful spacing. Auto-switches to light when `prefers-color-scheme: light`. Both themes are first-class (not a quick inversion).
28	
29	### Palette
30	
31	Defined in `apps/web/src/index.css` under Tailwind v4 `@theme`:
32	
33	| Token                | Dark      | Light     | Semantic               |
34	|----------------------|-----------|-----------|------------------------|
35	| `--color-bg`         | `#0b0d10` | `#ffffff` | page background        |
36	| `--color-surface`    | `#11141a` | `#f6f8fa` | cards, panels          |
37	| `--color-border`     | `#1d2125` | `#d0d7de` | hairline dividers      |
38	| `--color-text`       | `#e6e8eb` | `#1f2328` | body text              |
39	| `--color-text-dim`   | `#9ba1a6` | `#57606a` | secondary text         |
40	| `--color-text-faint` | `#7a8087` | `#8c959f` | tertiary / labels      |
41	| `--color-accent`     | `#3b82f6` | `#0969da` | focus, primary CTA     |
42	| `--color-success`    | `#22c55e` | `#1a7f37` | balance > 0 (reserved) |
43	| `--color-warn`       | `#f59e0b` | `#9a6700` | caution banners        |
44	| `--color-danger`     | `#f87171` | `#d1242f` | errors, locked state   |
45	
46	`--color-success` is reserved strictly for "balance found" and "key unlocked" moments — never for UI chrome like "action succeeded" toasts.
47	
48	### Type
49	
50	- **Sans:** `ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, sans-serif` — whole UI.
51	- **Mono:** `ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, monospace` — addresses, WIFs, derivation paths, tx hashes.
52	- **No serif anywhere.**
53	- Base size **14px** (was 18px). Labels 10–11px, `text-transform: uppercase`, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`. Headings 13 / 16 / 20 / 24px.
54	- All numeric cells use `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` so decimal points line up.
55	
56	### Spacing & shape
57	
58	- 4px base grid.
59	- Cards: 6px radius, 1px border, **no shadow**.
60	- Buttons: 5px radius.
61	- Focus ring: 2px solid `--color-accent`, no glow.
62	
63	### Motion
64	
65	- Remove existing `panel-enter`, `glyph-enter`, `descent-marker`, `grain`, `text-shadow` halo.
66	- Add: 120ms ease-out on hover backgrounds; 200ms fade on route/panel swap. Nothing else animates.
67	- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` — disable the fade.
68	
69	## 4. Information architecture
70	
71	### Top-level navigation
72	
73	Three tabs at top of page (the sidebar `Descent` nav is removed):
74	
75	- **Wallet files** (`/scan`)
76	- **Mnemonic** (`/mnemonic`)
77	- **Results** (`/results`)
78	
79	### Routes (React Router, client-side)
80	
81	| Route                         | Purpose                                                                |
82	|-------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
83	| `/`                           | redirects to `/scan`                                                   |
84	| `/scan`                       | file/folder input, password list, provider, submit                     |
85	| `/mnemonic`                   | phrase input, BIP39 vs blockchain.com, derive                          |
86	| `/results`                    | summary across all scans this session                                  |
87	| `/results/:walletId`          | one source file — addresses table, tabs for `All`/`With value`/`Empty` |
88	| `/results/:walletId/:address` | one address — balance, tx history, CTAs                                |
89	| `/import/:keyId`              | per-wallet import instructions (Electrum/Sparrow/Core/Hardware)        |
90	
91	- `walletId = sha256(source_file + '|' + source_type).slice(0, 16)`. Deterministic per scan input.
92	- `keyId = sha256(address).slice(0, 16)`. We never put WIFs in URLs.
93	
94	### Persistence
95	
96	Results cache in `sessionStorage` under key `orpheus:v1:results`. Cleared when the tab closes. No `localStorage`. No IndexedDB. No server-side storage.
97	
98	Schema is versioned. On schema-version mismatch, the cache is discarded without migration.
99	
100	### Global header
101	
102	On every route: brand mark · "Orpheus" wordmark · breadcrumbs for current route · right-aligned **active-provider indicator**.
103	
104	The indicator is functional, not decorative:
105	
106	- Green dot + name (e.g. `● blockstream.info`) when a network provider is selected.
107	- Amber dot when a non-default network provider is selected (`blockchain.info`).
108	- Grey dot + "offline" when provider = `mock` or `none`.
109	- Clicking the indicator opens a small provider-switcher popover.
110	
111	Goal: a user recovering a sensitive wallet can see at a glance whether balance lookups are hitting the network.
112	
113	## 5. Scan input methods
114	
115	Priority order, picked by runtime feature detection:
116	
117	1. **Tauri — native OS folder picker.** `@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog` `open({ directory: true, multiple: false })`. Path goes via a new `#[tauri::command] scan_directory(path, passwords, provider)` straight into `orpheus-core`. **No file transfer.** Highest privacy, best UX.
118	2. **Chrome/Edge — File System Access API.** `window.showDirectoryPicker({ startIn: 'documents' })` returns a `FileSystemDirectoryHandle`. We walk it recursively, collect candidate wallet files (`*.dat`, `*.wallet`, `*.aes.json`, `*.json`, `*.txt`), stream them to `POST /api/scan` as multipart. Server (running on `127.0.0.1`) does the extraction.
119	3. **Firefox / Safari / no FS Access — dropzone fallback.** Current multi-file drag-and-drop. Only rendered when the two above are unavailable.
120	
121	Feature detection runtime check, in order:
122	
123	```ts
124	if (window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__) return 'tauri';
125	if (typeof window.showDirectoryPicker === 'function') return 'fs-access';
126	return 'dropzone';
127	```
128	
129	### Scan page UX
130	
131	- Big primary button: **Pick a folder** (label: "Choose folder" in Tauri, "Pick folder" in Chrome). Expands into a multi-line help card showing common wallet paths by OS:
132	  - macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/Bitcoin/wallets/`
133	  - Linux: `~/.bitcoin/wallets/`
134	  - Windows: `%APPDATA%\Bitcoin\wallets\`
135	  - Plus `~/Documents` for legacy `.aes.json`, `.wallet` files.
136	- Thin link below: **"Or select individual files"** — reveals the dropzone fallback for any browser.
137	- Settings row (two columns on desktop): Provider `<select>` · Passwords `<textarea>` (monospace, placeholder `one per line`).
138	- Submit: **Scan**. Status line to the right: idle / `scanning… 47 of 103 files` / `done: 3 keys with value` / error message.
139	- Recent scans list (sessionStorage-backed) beneath the form, click to jump to `/results/:walletId`.
140	
141	## 6. Screens
142	
143	### `/mnemonic`
144	
145	- `Type` select: BIP39 / blockchain.com legacy.
146	- Phrase `<textarea>` (mono, 3 rows). Word-count indicator: `12 / 12 words` (green when 12/15/18/21/24; amber otherwise).
147	- Row: `BIP39 passphrase` (optional) · `Gap limit` (number, default 20, min 1, max 200).
148	- `wordlist` path input — rendered **only** when type = blockchain.com legacy.
149	- Submit: **Derive keys**. Status line.
150	- Decoded-password result card (blockchain.com legacy only): copyable, warning line "This password unlocks the `wallet.aes.json` payload."
151	
152	### `/results`
153	
154	- Sticky 4-stat bar at top: `Wallets` · `Keys` · `With value` · `Recovered BTC`. Last two coloured `--color-success` when non-zero.
155	- Per-wallet cards (clickable, navigate to `/results/:walletId`): filename, source-type pill (`bitcoin_core`, `multibit`, `encrypted`, `wallet_dump`, `bip39_mnemonic`, `blockchain_com`), subtotal row, top 3 address preview (with-balance first, else top 3 by path order).
156	- Empty state: "No scans this session. Start with [Wallet files] or [Mnemonic]." Links are real CTAs.
157	
158	### `/results/:walletId`
159	
160	- Breadcrumb: `Orpheus / Results / wallet.dat`.
161	- Header card: filename, source-type pill, total keys, keys-with-value, subtotal BTC.
162	- Inner tabs: `All (n)` · `With value (n)` · `Empty (n)`. Default to `With value` if any, else `All`.
163	- Table columns: Derivation pill (`BIP44` / `BIP49` / `BIP84` / `Bread`) · Address (mono, truncated with middle ellipsis) · BTC (right-aligned, `tabular-nums`, green when >0) · Txs (right-aligned) · `Open →` link.
164	- Sort by each column (click header). Search input filters over address + derivation path.
165	
166	### `/results/:walletId/:address`
167	
168	Two-column: detail (2fr) · actions (1fr).
169	
170	**Detail column:**
171	
172	- Label `Address`, full address in monospace + inline copy button.
173	- Big balance: 24px `tabular-nums`, green if >0, dim grey if 0.
174	- Fiat estimate line — rendered only when provider response includes a price; otherwise omitted. No fallback.
175	- 3-cell stat bar: `Derivation` (mono) · `Total received` · `Transactions`.
176	- Transactions card: list, each row = `txid` (mono, truncated, click → Blockstream) + date + direction (+/–) + value (green/red, `tabular-nums`) + confirmations. Empty state: "No transactions for this address."
177	
178	**Actions column (top-to-bottom):**
179	
180	- QR code card: SVG QR of the address (no network; library: `qrcode-generator`, ~2KB).
181	- WIF row (sensitive): locked by default, `••••••••••` placeholder. **Reveal** button shows the WIF and starts a 20-second clipboard-auto-clear timer if the user clicks **Copy**. No hover-to-reveal. No auto-reveal on navigation. Uses `@copied-at` timer, not page-level timer (survives re-render).
182	- CTA stack:
183	  - `Copy address`
184	  - `Open in Blockstream ↗` (external link, `rel="noopener noreferrer"`)
185	  - **`Import this key →`** (primary CTA, green) → navigates to `/import/:keyId`
186	  - `Export all keys (CSV / descriptor)` — secondary, downloads a file containing every derived key for the current wallet
187	
188	### `/import/:keyId`
189	
190	- Amber banner at top: "Handle on an air-gapped machine. Clear clipboard after use. Prefer sweeping funds to a new address once the key is imported."
191	- Tabs: `Electrum` · `Sparrow` · `Bitcoin Core` · `Hardware wallet` (Coldcard / Jade / Passport).
192	- Each tab: numbered steps as ordered list, with copyable commands/field values. Each tab ends with the WIF reveal row (same component as `/results/.../:address`).
193	- Bottom link: "Having trouble? See [`docs/RECOVERY.md`]" (the doc is a follow-up deliverable).
194	
195	### Global states
196	
197	- **Loading** (scan running): inline spinner + `Scanned 47 of 103 files`. No blocking modal.
198	- **Error**: inline banner above the relevant form or section. Red border, neutral surface bg. Includes a `Try again` action where recoverable.
199	- **Empty after scan**: "No keys extracted. The files may not be supported wallet formats — see the supported list →." Link to docs.
200	
201	## 7. Backend & core deltas
202	
203	### `orpheus-core` — balance provider trait
204	
205	Current method:
206	
207	```rust
208	fn balances(&self, addresses: &[&str]) -> Result<Vec<BalanceInfo>>
209	```
210	
211	Add:
212	
213	```rust
214	fn transactions(&self, address: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<Tx>>
215	
216	pub struct Tx {
217	    pub txid: String,
218	    pub time: i64,               // unix seconds, confirmed block time when available
219	    pub value_sat: i64,          // net effect on the query address:
220	                                 //   positive = received, negative = sent,
221	                                 //   zero = self-transfer (fee delta only)
222	    pub fee_sat: Option<u64>,
223	    pub confirmations: Option<u32>,
224	    pub block_height: Option<u64>,
225	}
226	```
227	
228	UI renders direction from `value_sat.signum()`; no separate `direction` field on the wire.
229	
230	Implementations:
231	
232	- **`BlockstreamProvider`:** `GET /address/:addr/txs` (Esplora). Paginated via `/chain/:last_seen_txid`. Native support.
233	- **`BlockchainInfoProvider`:** `GET /rawaddr/:addr?limit=50`. Parse into `Tx`.
234	- **`MockProvider`:** extend `fixtures/mock_balances.json` to include `transactions: [{ txid, time, value_sat, ... }]` per address. Deterministic test values.
235	- **`NoneProvider`:** returns `Ok(vec![])`.
236	
237	**Pinned tests per provider** using recorded HTTP fixtures (no live network): recorded response → parsed `Vec<Tx>` → asserted exact values. Same discipline as the rest of the core per `CLAUDE.md`.
238	
239	### `orpheus-server` — endpoints
240	
241	| Endpoint                                 | Status              | Notes                                                                         |
242	|------------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
243	| `POST /api/scan`                         | unchanged           | multipart upload, current behavior                                            |
244	| `POST /api/mnemonic`                     | unchanged           |                                                                               |
245	| `POST /api/demo`                         | unchanged           |                                                                               |
246	| `GET /api/address/:address/transactions` | **new**             | query params: `provider`, `limit` (default 50, max 200)                       |
247	| `POST /api/scan-directory`               | **new, flag-gated** | body `{ path, passwords, provider }`. See `--allow-local-paths` gate below.   |
248	
249	The `GET /api/address/:address/transactions` endpoint is an auth-free thin wrapper over the provider trait — acceptable because the server binds to `127.0.0.1` only.
250	
251	`POST /api/scan-directory` is **only mounted** when the server is started with the `--allow-local-paths` flag. When mounted, the path validator enforces:
252	
253	- Path must be absolute (rejects relative paths, rejects `~`).
254	- Path must not contain `..` segments (rejects traversal).
255	- Resolved path must be inside `$HOME` (rejects symlinks that escape home).
256	
257	There is no opt-out of the home restriction. A user who needs to scan outside `$HOME` uses the CLI directly.
258	
259	### `orpheus-tauri` — commands
260	
261	All call directly into `orpheus-core` without HTTP:
262	
263	```rust
264	#[tauri::command] async fn scan_directory(path: String, passwords: String, provider: String)
265	    -> Result<ScanResponse, String>
266	#[tauri::command] async fn mnemonic(payload: MnemonicRequest)
267	    -> Result<MnemonicResponse, String>
268	#[tauri::command] async fn address_transactions(address: String, provider: String, limit: usize)
269	    -> Result<Vec<Tx>, String>
270	```
271	
272	Web client detects Tauri via `window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__` and routes through these. A single adapter layer in `apps/web/src/lib/api.ts` hides the HTTP-vs-Tauri split so route components stay agnostic.
273	
274	### Provider list in sync
275	
276	Per `CLAUDE.md`: any provider change must land in the same PR across **three surfaces** — `orpheus-core::VALID_PROVIDERS`, clap `CliProvider`, frontend `<select>`. This redesign does not add or remove providers, but the `tauri-invoke` boundary becomes a fourth sync point for future changes.
277	
278	## 8. File & component structure
279	
280	```text
281	apps/web/src/
282	├── App.tsx                   — router shell
283	├── main.tsx                  — unchanged
284	├── index.css                 — @theme tokens + base reset only (no component CSS)
285	├── types.ts                  — extended with Tx, KeyId, WalletId
286	│
287	├── routes/
288	│   ├── ScanPage.tsx
289	│   ├── MnemonicPage.tsx
290	│   ├── ResultsPage.tsx
291	│   ├── WalletPage.tsx
292	│   ├── AddressPage.tsx
293	│   └── ImportPage.tsx
294	│
295	├── components/
296	│   ├── layout/
297	│   │   ├── Header.tsx        — brand + breadcrumbs + provider indicator
298	│   │   ├── Tabs.tsx
299	│   │   └── Breadcrumbs.tsx
300	│   ├── ui/                   — primitives (source of truth for visual style)
301	│   │   ├── Button.tsx
302	│   │   ├── Input.tsx
303	│   │   ├── Textarea.tsx
304	│   │   ├── Select.tsx
305	│   │   ├── Card.tsx
306	│   │   ├── Table.tsx
307	│   │   ├── Pill.tsx          — BIP44/49/84/Bread + source-type badges
308	│   │   ├── StatBar.tsx
309	│   │   ├── CopyButton.tsx
310	│   │   ├── WifReveal.tsx     — locked → reveal + auto-clear clipboard
311	│   │   ├── QRCode.tsx        — SVG, no network
312	│   │   ├── Spinner.tsx
313	│   │   └── Banner.tsx
314	│   ├── scan/
315	│   │   ├── FolderPicker.tsx  — runtime-detects Tauri / FS Access / fallback
316	│   │   ├── FileDropzone.tsx  — fallback
317	│   │   └── RecentScans.tsx
318	│   ├── results/
319	│   │   ├── WalletCard.tsx
320	│   │   ├── KeyTable.tsx
321	│   │   ├── TxTable.tsx
322	│   │   └── AddressDetail.tsx
323	│   └── import/
324	│       ├── ImportTabs.tsx
325	│       ├── ElectrumSteps.tsx
326	│       ├── SparrowSteps.tsx
327	│       ├── BitcoinCoreSteps.tsx
328	│       └── HardwareSteps.tsx
329	│
330	├── lib/
331	│   ├── api.ts                — Tauri/HTTP adapter (single interface)
332	│   ├── storage.ts            — sessionStorage wrapper, schema version
333	│   ├── ids.ts                — walletId/keyId derivation
334	│   ├── clipboard.ts          — copy + auto-clear timer
335	│   ├── fs-access.ts          — File System Access API walker
336	│   ├── tauri.ts              — feature detect + invoke wrappers
337	│   └── utils.ts              — cn, satToBtc, truncateMiddle, formatBytes
338	```
339	
340	### Deletions
341	
342	Rewritten structurally, so the following files are deleted rather than edited:
343	
344	- `apps/web/src/components/Descent.tsx`
345	- `apps/web/src/components/Masthead.tsx`
346	- `apps/web/src/components/Dropzone.tsx`
347	- `apps/web/src/components/Field.tsx`
348	- `apps/web/src/components/ResultsView.tsx`
349	
350	### New dependencies (web)
351	
352	- `react-router-dom` — client-side routing.
353	- `qrcode-generator` — 2KB, SVG output, no network.
354	- `@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog` — Tauri only, folder picker.
355	- `@testing-library/react`, `@testing-library/user-event`, `@testing-library/jest-dom` — dev-only, component tests.
356	
357	No component library. All UI primitives hand-rolled against Tailwind v4.
358	
359	## 9. Testing
360	
361	- **UI primitives** (`components/ui/`): unit tests with Testing Library. Focus: `WifReveal` (clipboard + auto-clear timing), `CopyButton`, `QRCode` (renders correct SVG for a known address), `Table` (sort / filter behavior), `Banner` (variants).
362	- **Route-level integration**: one test per route rendering with a mocked `api.ts`, asserts key content and CTAs. Not visual/screenshot tests.
363	- **`lib/`**: `ids.ts` (hash stability + known-input pinned values), `storage.ts` (schema version + mismatch discard), `fs-access.ts` (walker given a mock directory handle), `tauri.ts` (feature-detect matrix), `clipboard.ts` (auto-clear timing).
364	- **Provider tx-parsing** (Rust): pinned fixture per provider. Recorded HTTP → `Vec<Tx>` → exact-value assertions. `MockProvider` fixture extension covered by extending the existing mock-balances test.
365	- **`scan-directory` path validator** (Rust): unit tests for absolute-path requirement, `..` traversal rejection, symlink-to-outside-home rejection.
366	
367	Per project rules: no real wallets, real mnemonics, or real passwords in any new test. Fixtures via `orpheus-demo-fixtures` or inline generation.
368	
369	## 10. Accessibility
370	
371	- All interactive elements keyboard-reachable. Visible 2px focus ring on every focusable control.
372	- Tabs: `role="tablist"` / `role="tab"` / `aria-selected` / arrow-key navigation.
373	- Tables: `<th scope="col">`; sort controls announce state with `aria-sort`.
374	- WIF row hidden state: `aria-label="Private key (hidden — activate reveal to show)"`.
375	- Color contrast: both themes hit WCAG AA for body text, UI text, and CTA buttons. Verified in CI via a contrast check (added to `mise run ci`).
376	- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.
377	- Route transitions: `<main>` receives focus on route change; breadcrumb changes are announced via `aria-live="polite"`.
378	
379	## 11. Explicit punts (follow-ups, not this PR)
380	
381	- **Fiat price feed.** Render-only when already present in a provider response.
382	- **Per-transaction detail view inside Orpheus.** Click a tx → Blockstream.
383	- **Command palette (⌘K).**
384	- **Settings page.** Provider lives in the header popover.
385	- **Sweep / sign / broadcast.** Hard line — out of scope forever, not just this PR.
386	- **`docs/RECOVERY.md`.** Referenced by the Import page footer; a placeholder link is acceptable in the first merge.
387	
388	## 12. Ship order
389	
390	The implementation plan (next artifact) will be a checklist; this is the dependency order:
391	
392	1. Worktree + branch + `.gitignore` entry for `.superpowers/`. *(done — this branch)*
393	2. Tokens + base CSS reset — fixes the broken-vars bug; everything else depends on this.
394	3. UI primitives (Button / Input / Textarea / Select / Card / Table / Pill / StatBar / CopyButton / WifReveal / QRCode / Spinner / Banner).
395	4. Router + layout shell (Header / Tabs / Breadcrumbs).
396	5. `ScanPage` + `FolderPicker` + FS Access walker + Tauri adapter layer.
397	6. `MnemonicPage`.
398	7. **Backend work:** provider trait `transactions()` + server endpoints + Tauri commands + pinned fixture tests.
399	8. `ResultsPage` / `WalletPage` / `AddressPage`.
400	9. `ImportPage` + per-wallet instruction components.
401	10. A11y + contrast audit + test coverage + delete old components.
402	11. Manual smoke: `mise run server:dev` in Chrome + Firefox; `mise run tauri:dev` on macOS.
403	
404	## 13. Open questions
405	
406	None blocking. Follow-ups tracked in §11.
407

file.content:
# Orpheus web app redesign — design spec

- **Date:** 2026-04-17
- **Branch:** `feat/web-redesign`
- **Scope:** `apps/web/**`, plus targeted extensions in `orpheus-core`, `orpheus-server`, `orpheus-tauri/src-tauri` to support the new UI.
- **Out of scope:** extractor logic, crypto code, CLI (except kept in sync per project rules).

## 1. Goals

1. **Fix broken CSS.** Every component references `--color-bronze`, `--color-verdigris`, `--color-rust`, `--color-fg`, `--color-fg-dim`, `--color-fg-faint`, `--color-rule`, `--color-bg-inset`, `--color-bronze-lit`. **None are defined** — `apps/web/src/index.css` contains only unrelated starter tokens (`--text`, `--bg`, `--border`, `--accent`). The app renders on browser defaults. This is the first thing to fix.
2. **Strip the Greek-mythology theming.** The name **Orpheus** stays. Everything else goes: Ω glyph, `καταβαίνω`, "to descend", "Descent" navigation, Roman numerals (I / II / III / IV) as panel numbers, "Cast the phrase", "Begin descent", "silence in the hall", "Retrieved from the underworld", "a warning from the guide", serif display type, bronze/verdigris/rust palette, `grain` overlay, `text-shadow` glow halos, `panel-enter`/`glyph-enter`/`descent-marker` animations.
3. **Turn the app into a navigable explorer.** Today: "form → static result list." Target: bookmarkable routes for results / wallet / address / import-instructions with clear CTAs that end on *"I have my key and I know how to use it."*

## 2. Non-goals

- No changes to extractor logic, crypto routines, or `orpheus-core::balance::VALID_PROVIDERS` membership.
- No Tauri shell redesign beyond the new `#[tauri::command]` endpoints needed for folder-picking and transaction lookups.
- No keyboard shortcut palette (⌘K).
- No sweep/sign/broadcast in-app. Orpheus extracts and explains. It does not transact. That boundary is non-negotiable.
- No fiat price fetching. Render a USD estimate only when the balance provider response happens to include a price; otherwise omit. No new price feed.
- No server-side persistence of scan results. Session-scoped only.

## 3. Visual system

### Style

Linear/Vercel-adjacent: flat, dark-default, sans-serif, one accent color, generous-but-not-wasteful spacing. Auto-switches to light when `prefers-color-scheme: light`. Both themes are first-class (not a quick inversion).

### Palette

Defined in `apps/web/src/index.css` under Tailwind v4 `@theme`:

| Token                | Dark      | Light     | Semantic               |
|----------------------|-----------|-----------|------------------------|
| `--color-bg`         | `#0b0d10` | `#ffffff` | page background        |
| `--color-surface`    | `#11141a` | `#f6f8fa` | cards, panels          |
| `--color-border`     | `#1d2125` | `#d0d7de` | hairline dividers      |
| `--color-text`       | `#e6e8eb` | `#1f2328` | body text              |
| `--color-text-dim`   | `#9ba1a6` | `#57606a` | secondary text         |
| `--color-text-faint` | `#7a8087` | `#8c959f` | tertiary / labels      |
| `--color-accent`     | `#3b82f6` | `#0969da` | focus, primary CTA     |
| `--color-success`    | `#22c55e` | `#1a7f37` | balance > 0 (reserved) |
| `--color-warn`       | `#f59e0b` | `#9a6700` | caution banners        |
| `--color-danger`     | `#f87171` | `#d1242f` | errors, locked state   |

`--color-success` is reserved strictly for "balance found" and "key unlocked" moments — never for UI chrome like "action succeeded" toasts.

### Type

- **Sans:** `ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, sans-serif` — whole UI.
- **Mono:** `ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, monospace` — addresses, WIFs, derivation paths, tx hashes.
- **No serif anywhere.**
- Base size **14px** (was 18px). Labels 10–11px, `text-transform: uppercase`, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`. Headings 13 / 16 / 20 / 24px.
- All numeric cells use `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` so decimal points line up.

### Spacing & shape

- 4px base grid.
- Cards: 6px radius, 1px border, **no shadow**.
- Buttons: 5px radius.
- Focus ring: 2px solid `--color-accent`, no glow.

### Motion

- Remove existing `panel-enter`, `glyph-enter`, `descent-marker`, `grain`, `text-shadow` halo.
- Add: 120ms ease-out on hover backgrounds; 200ms fade on route/panel swap. Nothing else animates.
- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` — disable the fade.

## 4. Information architecture

### Top-level navigation

Three tabs at top of page (the sidebar `Descent` nav is removed):

- **Wallet files** (`/scan`)
- **Mnemonic** (`/mnemonic`)
- **Results** (`/results`)

### Routes (React Router, client-side)

| Route                         | Purpose                                                                |
|-------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `/`                           | redirects to `/scan`                                                   |
| `/scan`                       | file/folder input, password list, provider, submit                     |
| `/mnemonic`                   | phrase input, BIP39 vs blockchain.com, derive                          |
| `/results`                    | summary across all scans this session                                  |
| `/results/:walletId`          | one source file — addresses table, tabs for `All`/`With value`/`Empty` |
| `/results/:walletId/:address` | one address — balance, tx history, CTAs                                |
| `/import/:keyId`              | per-wallet import instructions (Electrum/Sparrow/Core/Hardware)        |

- `walletId = sha256(source_file + '|' + source_type).slice(0, 16)`. Deterministic per scan input.
- `keyId = sha256(address).slice(0, 16)`. We never put WIFs in URLs.

### Persistence

Results cache in `sessionStorage` under key `orpheus:v1:results`. Cleared when the tab closes. No `localStorage`. No IndexedDB. No server-side storage.

Schema is versioned. On schema-version mismatch, the cache is discarded without migration.

### Global header

On every route: brand mark · "Orpheus" wordmark · breadcrumbs for current route · right-aligned **active-provider indicator**.

The indicator is functional, not decorative:

- Green dot + name (e.g. `● blockstream.info`) when a network provider is selected.
- Amber dot when a non-default network provider is selected (`blockchain.info`).
- Grey dot + "offline" when provider = `mock` or `none`.
- Clicking the indicator opens a small provider-switcher popover.

Goal: a user recovering a sensitive wallet can see at a glance whether balance lookups are hitting the network.

## 5. Scan input methods

Priority order, picked by runtime feature detection:

1. **Tauri — native OS folder picker.** `@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog` `open({ directory: true, multiple: false })`. Path goes via a new `#[tauri::command] scan_directory(path, passwords, provider)` straight into `orpheus-core`. **No file transfer.** Highest privacy, best UX.
2. **Chrome/Edge — File System Access API.** `window.showDirectoryPicker({ startIn: 'documents' })` returns a `FileSystemDirectoryHandle`. We walk it recursively, collect candidate wallet files (`*.dat`, `*.wallet`, `*.aes.json`, `*.json`, `*.txt`), stream them to `POST /api/scan` as multipart. Server (running on `127.0.0.1`) does the extraction.
3. **Firefox / Safari / no FS Access — dropzone fallback.** Current multi-file drag-and-drop. Only rendered when the two above are unavailable.

Feature detection runtime check, in order:

```ts
if (window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__) return 'tauri';
if (typeof window.showDirectoryPicker === 'function') return 'fs-access';
return 'dropzone';
```

### Scan page UX

- Big primary button: **Pick a folder** (label: "Choose folder" in Tauri, "Pick folder" in Chrome). Expands into a multi-line help card showing common wallet paths by OS:
  - macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/Bitcoin/wallets/`
  - Linux: `~/.bitcoin/wallets/`
  - Windows: `%APPDATA%\Bitcoin\wallets\`
  - Plus `~/Documents` for legacy `.aes.json`, `.wallet` files.
- Thin link below: **"Or select individual files"** — reveals the dropzone fallback for any browser.
- Settings row (two columns on desktop): Provider `<select>` · Passwords `<textarea>` (monospace, placeholder `one per line`).
- Submit: **Scan**. Status line to the right: idle / `scanning… 47 of 103 files` / `done: 3 keys with value` / error message.
- Recent scans list (sessionStorage-backed) beneath the form, click to jump to `/results/:walletId`.

## 6. Screens

### `/mnemonic`

- `Type` select: BIP39 / blockchain.com legacy.
- Phrase `<textarea>` (mono, 3 rows). Word-count indicator: `12 / 12 words` (green when 12/15/18/21/24; amber otherwise).
- Row: `BIP39 passphrase` (optional) · `Gap limit` (number, default 20, min 1, max 200).
- `wordlist` path input — rendered **only** when type = blockchain.com legacy.
- Submit: **Derive keys**. Status line.
- Decoded-password result card (blockchain.com legacy only): copyable, warning line "This password unlocks the `wallet.aes.json` payload."

### `/results`

- Sticky 4-stat bar at top: `Wallets` · `Keys` · `With value` · `Recovered BTC`. Last two coloured `--color-success` when non-zero.
- Per-wallet cards (clickable, navigate to `/results/:walletId`): filename, source-type pill (`bitcoin_core`, `multibit`, `encrypted`, `wallet_dump`, `bip39_mnemonic`, `blockchain_com`), subtotal row, top 3 address preview (with-balance first, else top 3 by path order).
- Empty state: "No scans this session. Start with [Wallet files] or [Mnemonic]." Links are real CTAs.

### `/results/:walletId`

- Breadcrumb: `Orpheus / Results / wallet.dat`.
- Header card: filename, source-type pill, total keys, keys-with-value, subtotal BTC.
- Inner tabs: `All (n)` · `With value (n)` · `Empty (n)`. Default to `With value` if any, else `All`.
- Table columns: Derivation pill (`BIP44` / `BIP49` / `BIP84` / `Bread`) · Address (mono, truncated with middle ellipsis) · BTC (right-aligned, `tabular-nums`, green when >0) · Txs (right-aligned) · `Open →` link.
- Sort by each column (click header). Search input filters over address + derivation path.

### `/results/:walletId/:address`

Two-column: detail (2fr) · actions (1fr).

**Detail column:**

- Label `Address`, full address in monospace + inline copy button.
- Big balance: 24px `tabular-nums`, green if >0, dim grey if 0.
- Fiat estimate line — rendered only when provider response includes a price; otherwise omitted. No fallback.
- 3-cell stat bar: `Derivation` (mono) · `Total received` · `Transactions`.
- Transactions card: list, each row = `txid` (mono, truncated, click → Blockstream) + date + direction (+/–) + value (green/red, `tabular-nums`) + confirmations. Empty state: "No transactions for this address."

**Actions column (top-to-bottom):**

- QR code card: SVG QR of the address (no network; library: `qrcode-generator`, ~2KB).
- WIF row (sensitive): locked by default, `••••••••••` placeholder. **Reveal** button shows the WIF and starts a 20-second clipboard-auto-clear timer if the user clicks **Copy**. No hover-to-reveal. No auto-reveal on navigation. Uses `@copied-at` timer, not page-level timer (survives re-render).
- CTA stack:
  - `Copy address`
  - `Open in Blockstream ↗` (external link, `rel="noopener noreferrer"`)
  - **`Import this key →`** (primary CTA, green) → navigates to `/import/:keyId`
  - `Export all keys (CSV / descriptor)` — secondary, downloads a file containing every derived key for the current wallet

### `/import/:keyId`

- Amber banner at top: "Handle on an air-gapped machine. Clear clipboard after use. Prefer sweeping funds to a new address once the key is imported."
- Tabs: `Electrum` · `Sparrow` · `Bitcoin Core` · `Hardware wallet` (Coldcard / Jade / Passport).
- Each tab: numbered steps as ordered list, with copyable commands/field values. Each tab ends with the WIF reveal row (same component as `/results/.../:address`).
- Bottom link: "Having trouble? See [`docs/RECOVERY.md`]" (the doc is a follow-up deliverable).

### Global states

- **Loading** (scan running): inline spinner + `Scanned 47 of 103 files`. No blocking modal.
- **Error**: inline banner above the relevant form or section. Red border, neutral surface bg. Includes a `Try again` action where recoverable.
- **Empty after scan**: "No keys extracted. The files may not be supported wallet formats — see the supported list →." Link to docs.

## 7. Backend & core deltas

### `orpheus-core` — balance provider trait

Current method:

```rust
fn balances(&self, addresses: &[&str]) -> Result<Vec<BalanceInfo>>
```

Add:

```rust
fn transactions(&self, address: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<Tx>>

pub struct Tx {
    pub txid: String,
    pub time: i64,               // unix seconds, confirmed block time when available
    pub value_sat: i64,          // net effect on the query address:
                                 //   positive = received, negative = sent,
                                 //   zero = self-transfer (fee delta only)
    pub fee_sat: Option<u64>,
    pub confirmations: Option<u32>,
    pub block_height: Option<u64>,
}
```

UI renders direction from `value_sat.signum()`; no separate `direction` field on the wire.

Implementations:

- **`BlockstreamProvider`:** `GET /address/:addr/txs` (Esplora). Paginated via `/chain/:last_seen_txid`. Native support.
- **`BlockchainInfoProvider`:** `GET /rawaddr/:addr?limit=50`. Parse into `Tx`.
- **`MockProvider`:** extend `fixtures/mock_balances.json` to include `transactions: [{ txid, time, value_sat, ... }]` per address. Deterministic test values.
- **`NoneProvider`:** returns `Ok(vec![])`.

**Pinned tests per provider** using recorded HTTP fixtures (no live network): recorded response → parsed `Vec<Tx>` → asserted exact values. Same discipline as the rest of the core per `CLAUDE.md`.

### `orpheus-server` — endpoints

| Endpoint                                 | Status              | Notes                                                                         |
|------------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `POST /api/scan`                         | unchanged           | multipart upload, current behavior                                            |
| `POST /api/mnemonic`                     | unchanged           |                                                                               |
| `POST /api/demo`                         | unchanged           |                                                                               |
| `GET /api/address/:address/transactions` | **new**             | query params: `provider`, `limit` (default 50, max 200)                       |
| `POST /api/scan-directory`               | **new, flag-gated** | body `{ path, passwords, provider }`. See `--allow-local-paths` gate below.   |

The `GET /api/address/:address/transactions` endpoint is an auth-free thin wrapper over the provider trait — acceptable because the server binds to `127.0.0.1` only.

`POST /api/scan-directory` is **only mounted** when the server is started with the `--allow-local-paths` flag. When mounted, the path validator enforces:

- Path must be absolute (rejects relative paths, rejects `~`).
- Path must not contain `..` segments (rejects traversal).
- Resolved path must be inside `$HOME` (rejects symlinks that escape home).

There is no opt-out of the home restriction. A user who needs to scan outside `$HOME` uses the CLI directly.

### `orpheus-tauri` — commands

All call directly into `orpheus-core` without HTTP:

```rust
#[tauri::command] async fn scan_directory(path: String, passwords: String, provider: String)
    -> Result<ScanResponse, String>
#[tauri::command] async fn mnemonic(payload: MnemonicRequest)
    -> Result<MnemonicResponse, String>
#[tauri::command] async fn address_transactions(address: String, provider: String, limit: usize)
    -> Result<Vec<Tx>, String>
```

Web client detects Tauri via `window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__` and routes through these. A single adapter layer in `apps/web/src/lib/api.ts` hides the HTTP-vs-Tauri split so route components stay agnostic.

### Provider list in sync

Per `CLAUDE.md`: any provider change must land in the same PR across **three surfaces** — `orpheus-core::VALID_PROVIDERS`, clap `CliProvider`, frontend `<select>`. This redesign does not add or remove providers, but the `tauri-invoke` boundary becomes a fourth sync point for future changes.

## 8. File & component structure

```text
apps/web/src/
├── App.tsx                   — router shell
├── main.tsx                  — unchanged
├── index.css                 — @theme tokens + base reset only (no component CSS)
├── types.ts                  — extended with Tx, KeyId, WalletId
│
├── routes/
│   ├── ScanPage.tsx
│   ├── MnemonicPage.tsx
│   ├── ResultsPage.tsx
│   ├── WalletPage.tsx
│   ├── AddressPage.tsx
│   └── ImportPage.tsx
│
├── components/
│   ├── layout/
│   │   ├── Header.tsx        — brand + breadcrumbs + provider indicator
│   │   ├── Tabs.tsx
│   │   └── Breadcrumbs.tsx
│   ├── ui/                   — primitives (source of truth for visual style)
│   │   ├── Button.tsx
│   │   ├── Input.tsx
│   │   ├── Textarea.tsx
│   │   ├── Select.tsx
│   │   ├── Card.tsx
│   │   ├── Table.tsx
│   │   ├── Pill.tsx          — BIP44/49/84/Bread + source-type badges
│   │   ├── StatBar.tsx
│   │   ├── CopyButton.tsx
│   │   ├── WifReveal.tsx     — locked → reveal + auto-clear clipboard
│   │   ├── QRCode.tsx        — SVG, no network
│   │   ├── Spinner.tsx
│   │   └── Banner.tsx
│   ├── scan/
│   │   ├── FolderPicker.tsx  — runtime-detects Tauri / FS Access / fallback
│   │   ├── FileDropzone.tsx  — fallback
│   │   └── RecentScans.tsx
│   ├── results/
│   │   ├── WalletCard.tsx
│   │   ├── KeyTable.tsx
│   │   ├── TxTable.tsx
│   │   └── AddressDetail.tsx
│   └── import/
│       ├── ImportTabs.tsx
│       ├── ElectrumSteps.tsx
│       ├── SparrowSteps.tsx
│       ├── BitcoinCoreSteps.tsx
│       └── HardwareSteps.tsx
│
├── lib/
│   ├── api.ts                — Tauri/HTTP adapter (single interface)
│   ├── storage.ts            — sessionStorage wrapper, schema version
│   ├── ids.ts                — walletId/keyId derivation
│   ├── clipboard.ts          — copy + auto-clear timer
│   ├── fs-access.ts          — File System Access API walker
│   ├── tauri.ts              — feature detect + invoke wrappers
│   └── utils.ts              — cn, satToBtc, truncateMiddle, formatBytes
```

### Deletions

Rewritten structurally, so the following files are deleted rather than edited:

- `apps/web/src/components/Descent.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/components/Masthead.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/components/Dropzone.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/components/Field.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/components/ResultsView.tsx`

### New dependencies (web)

- `react-router-dom` — client-side routing.
- `qrcode-generator` — 2KB, SVG output, no network.
- `@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog` — Tauri only, folder picker.
- `@testing-library/react`, `@testing-library/user-event`, `@testing-library/jest-dom` — dev-only, component tests.

No component library. All UI primitives hand-rolled against Tailwind v4.

## 9. Testing

- **UI primitives** (`components/ui/`): unit tests with Testing Library. Focus: `WifReveal` (clipboard + auto-clear timing), `CopyButton`, `QRCode` (renders correct SVG for a known address), `Table` (sort / filter behavior), `Banner` (variants).
- **Route-level integration**: one test per route rendering with a mocked `api.ts`, asserts key content and CTAs. Not visual/screenshot tests.
- **`lib/`**: `ids.ts` (hash stability + known-input pinned values), `storage.ts` (schema version + mismatch discard), `fs-access.ts` (walker given a mock directory handle), `tauri.ts` (feature-detect matrix), `clipboard.ts` (auto-clear timing).
- **Provider tx-parsing** (Rust): pinned fixture per provider. Recorded HTTP → `Vec<Tx>` → exact-value assertions. `MockProvider` fixture extension covered by extending the existing mock-balances test.
- **`scan-directory` path validator** (Rust): unit tests for absolute-path requirement, `..` traversal rejection, symlink-to-outside-home rejection.

Per project rules: no real wallets, real mnemonics, or real passwords in any new test. Fixtures via `orpheus-demo-fixtures` or inline generation.

## 10. Accessibility

- All interactive elements keyboard-reachable. Visible 2px focus ring on every focusable control.
- Tabs: `role="tablist"` / `role="tab"` / `aria-selected` / arrow-key navigation.
- Tables: `<th scope="col">`; sort controls announce state with `aria-sort`.
- WIF row hidden state: `aria-label="Private key (hidden — activate reveal to show)"`.
- Color contrast: both themes hit WCAG AA for body text, UI text, and CTA buttons. Verified in CI via a contrast check (added to `mise run ci`).
- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- Route transitions: `<main>` receives focus on route change; breadcrumb changes are announced via `aria-live="polite"`.

## 11. Explicit punts (follow-ups, not this PR)

- **Fiat price feed.** Render-only when already present in a provider response.
- **Per-transaction detail view inside Orpheus.** Click a tx → Blockstream.
- **Command palette (⌘K).**
- **Settings page.** Provider lives in the header popover.
- **Sweep / sign / broadcast.** Hard line — out of scope forever, not just this PR.
- **`docs/RECOVERY.md`.** Referenced by the Import page footer; a placeholder link is acceptable in the first merge.

## 12. Ship order

The implementation plan (next artifact) will be a checklist; this is the dependency order:

1. Worktree + branch + `.gitignore` entry for `.superpowers/`. *(done — this branch)*
2. Tokens + base CSS reset — fixes the broken-vars bug; everything else depends on this.
3. UI primitives (Button / Input / Textarea / Select / Card / Table / Pill / StatBar / CopyButton / WifReveal / QRCode / Spinner / Banner).
4. Router + layout shell (Header / Tabs / Breadcrumbs).
5. `ScanPage` + `FolderPicker` + FS Access walker + Tauri adapter layer.
6. `MnemonicPage`.
7. **Backend work:** provider trait `transactions()` + server endpoints + Tauri commands + pinned fixture tests.
8. `ResultsPage` / `WalletPage` / `AddressPage`.
9. `ImportPage` + per-wallet instruction components.
10. A11y + contrast audit + test coverage + delete old components.
11. Manual smoke: `mise run server:dev` in Chrome + Firefox; `mise run tauri:dev` on macOS.

## 13. Open questions

None blocking. Follow-ups tracked in §11.

{
  "name": "web",
  "private": true,
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "tsc -b && vite build",
    "lint": "eslint .",
    "format": "prettier --write .",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "typecheck": "tsc -b --noEmit"
  },
  "packageManager": "pnpm@9.15.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.2.2",
    "@tauri-apps/api": "^2.0.0",
    "class-variance-authority": "^0.7.1",
    "clsx": "^2.1.1",
    "lucide-react": "^1.8.0",
    "qrcode-generator": "^1.4.4",
    "react": "^19.2.4",
    "react-dom": "^19.2.4",

gh pr create --draft --base main --head feat/web-redesign-ui-primitives --title "feat(web): Phase 2 — UI primitives" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Implements **Phase 2** of the web redesign plan ([docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-web-redesign.md](../blob/main/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-web-redesign.md)). Phase 2 adds seven hand-rolled UI primitives under `apps/web/src/components/ui/`, each with colocated Vitest + Testing Library tests. No component library — everything is built against Tailwind v4 tokens (defined in Phase 1).

Phase 0 and Phase 1 merged in #9. Phase 3 (routing + Header + Breadcrumbs) is the follow-up.

## Tasks

- [x] **2.1** Button (4 variants)
- [ ] **2.2** Input / Textarea / Select / Field
- [ ] **2.3** Card / Pill / StatBar / Spinner / Banner
- [ ] **2.4** CopyButton
- [ ] **2.5** WifReveal (auto-clear clipboard)
- [ ] **2.6** QRCode (SVG, `qrcode-generator`, JSX rects — no innerHTML)
- [ ] **2.7** Table (click-to-sort)

Each task ships via TDD: failing test → implementation → green → commit.

## Test plan

- [x] \`pnpm -C apps/web run test\` green for every primitive
- [ ] \`pnpm -C apps/web run typecheck\` clean
- [ ] \`pnpm -C apps/web run lint\` clean (note: one pre-existing error in App.tsx is scheduled for Phase 3 Task 3.1)
- [ ] \`mise run ci\` passes locally before marking ready for review

Built with Claude Code using the superpowers:subagent-driven-development skill.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

You are reviewing whether a Button primitive implementation matches its specification.

## Where the code lives

The implementation is in a git worktree:
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives`
- Branch: `feat/web-redesign-ui-primitives`
- Commit under review: `66ad777` (HEAD)

Inspect the actual files there, not the main repo path.

## What Was Requested (Task 2.1 from docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-web-redesign.md)

A hand-rolled Button React component with:
- Variant prop supporting: `primary` (default), `secondary`, `success`, `ghost`
- Tailwind classes referencing CSS variables (e.g. `--color-accent`, `--color-border`, `--color-success`)
- Extends native `<button>` props via `ComponentProps<"button">`
- Uses `cn` helper from `@/lib/utils`
- Default props: `variant="primary"`, accepts `className` override

Test requirements (5 tests):
1. Renders children and fires onClick
2. Does not fire onClick when disabled
3. Applies `bg-[var(--color-accent)]` by default (primary)
4. Applies `border-[var(--color-border)]` for secondary
5. Applies `bg-[var(--color-success)]` for success

Full plan text matches the exact code snippets in the task. Verify the code matches *exactly* — this is a TDD task with prescribed code.

## What Implementer Claims

- Created `apps/web/src/components/ui/Button.tsx` and `Button.test.tsx`
- 5 tests passing
- Typecheck clean
- Flagged a pre-existing lint error in `apps/web/src/App.tsx:54` — says it was in the base commit `d45f1c3`, not their work

## Your Job

Do NOT trust the claims. Read the actual code and verify:

1. `apps/web/src/components/ui/Button.tsx` and `Button.test.tsx` exist in the worktree
2. The code matches the spec from the plan verbatim (variant names, class strings, structure)
3. Nothing else was modified in the commit (only the two expected files)
4. The claim about the pre-existing lint error is accurate: is `App.tsx:54` identical between `origin/main` and the current branch? If yes, it's out of scope for this task.

Commands to use:

```bash
cat apps/web/src/components/ui/Button.tsx
cat apps/web/src/components/ui/Button.test.tsx
git diff origin/main -- apps/web/src/App.tsx  # should be empty if implementer is right
```

- ✅ Spec compliant — or
- ❌ Issues found: list specifically, with file:line references

content.text:
## Code Review: Task 2.1 — Button primitive

**Files reviewed:**
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/Button.tsx` (31 lines)
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/Button.test.tsx` (39 lines)

### Strengths

- **Tidy single-responsibility component.** Pure presentational wrapper around `<button>` — no side effects, no internal state, no imperative handles. Exactly the "hand-rolled primitive" the plan asks for.
- **Variant-record pattern is correctly applied.** `Record<Variant, string>` keyed by the `Variant` union gives exhaustive type-checking: adding a new variant to the union forces a map entry. Good use of the type system.
- **Props spread is ordered safely.** `{...rest}` comes before `className`, so the caller cannot accidentally overwrite the composed variant classes. `cn(base, variants[variant], className)` also allows a caller to *append* overrides without losing base styling — the right precedence.
- **Token-based Tailwind classes match Phase 1 convention.** All colors go through `bg-[var(--color-*)]`, no hardcoded hex or `@apply`.
- **Tests exercise behavior, not implementation.** `userEvent.click` with a spy asserts the real DOM interaction; the disabled test asserts the user-observable outcome (handler doesn't fire) rather than probing a `disabled` attribute. That's the right bar.
- **Uses `getByRole("button", { name: ... })`** — accessibility-first query, not a brittle class/text selector.
- **File size is appropriate.** 31 + 39 lines is in the sweet spot for a primitive; nothing is overgrown.

### Issues

**Important (80-89):**

None rise to this bar for a presentational primitive.

### Minor observations (below the 80-confidence reporting threshold — sharing as FYI only, not blockers)

- The three "applies the X variant class" tests assert a single representative Tailwind class per variant. That's a reasonable proxy, but it does couple tests to the exact class string — refactoring `bg-[var(--color-accent)]` to a custom Tailwind utility would break them without a behavior change. Acceptable tradeoff for a primitive; flagging in case it bites later.
- `ghost` variant has no dedicated test (primary/secondary/success do). Low-value to add given variants is a typed `Record`, but noting for symmetry.
- `hover:opacity-90` on `success` is stylistically inconsistent with the other variants' explicit hover colors — if you add a `--color-success-lit` token later, aligning this would be cleaner. Not worth blocking on.

None of these violate CLAUDE.md or the plan.

### Accessibility

- Native `<button>` element preserves default keyboard semantics (Enter/Space activation, focus ring, disabled behavior) — correct choice over a `<div role="button">`.
- `disabled` is forwarded via `...rest`, so `aria-disabled` is handled implicitly by the native attribute.
- No visible focus style override — defaults kick in. Good.
- No missing `type="button"` default — worth noting that inside a `<form>` this button will submit by default. Not a bug per se (matches native semantics, and callers can set `type` via `...rest`), but a common footgun. Flagging as an observation, not a change request.

### Assessment

**Approved.**

Clean, minimal, well-tested primitive that follows the plan's variant-record pattern, respects Phase 1's `@theme` token convention, and uses accessibility-first test queries. No critical or important issues. Safe to merge.

content.text:
## Code Review: Task 2.2 — FormFields primitives

Reviewing commit `63f8a1d` (2 files, +104 LoC):
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/FormFields.tsx`
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/FormFields.test.tsx`

### Strengths

- **Small, cohesive surface.** Five exports sharing one `control` string is exactly the justification given for grouping — no premature one-file-per-component overhead.
- **Prop forwarding is correct.** `{...props}` before `className={cn(control, props.className)}` deliberately lets `cn`/`twMerge` win the last-word merge. With `tailwind-merge` in `cn`, a caller passing e.g. `className="px-4"` will correctly override the `px-3` from the `control` string rather than producing duplicate classes. Good use of the existing helper.
- **Sensible `Textarea` default.** `rows={3}` placed before `{...props}` so callers can override (verified by the `rows={4}` test).
- **Typography discipline matches the product.** `font-mono` on `Input`/`Textarea` (addresses, WIFs) and `font-sans` override on `Select` is the right call for a key-recovery UI.
- **Accessibility of `Field` is implicit-association correct.** Wrapping the control in `<label>` gives programmatic label→input association without needing `htmlFor`/`id` wiring. The test confirms both `getByText("Provider")` and `getByRole("textbox")` resolve.
- **Tests are behavior-focused, not implementation-focused** — uses `getByRole("textbox")`, `getByRole("combobox")`, no snapshot noise, no internal-class assertions.

### Issues

**None at confidence ≥ 80.**

Notes I considered and rejected:

- *The `Input` onChange test asserts `captured === "i"` rather than `"hi"`.* This is intentional — `captured` is a plain variable, not state, so the uncontrolled `value=""` prop keeps resetting the DOM and each keystroke replays from empty. The test still proves `onChange` fires with the typed character. It would be clearer as a controlled component test, but it isn't wrong. Confidence ~55, below threshold.
- *`FieldLabel` uses `<span>` inside a `<label>`.* Valid HTML, renders inline, and `Field` provides the block layout via `flex flex-col`. Not an issue.
- *No explicit `Field` test for label-input association via `getByLabelText`.* Would be a stronger a11y regression guard than `getByText`, but the current assertions are sufficient; confidence ~50.
- *Focus ring uses `focus:border-*` only (no `focus:ring`/`outline`).* That's a deliberate Linear-style aesthetic choice from the plan, not a bug.
- No `any`, no `console.log`, no single-letter identifiers in violation of typical repo rules; the test uses `ta`/`sel` as locals which is fine.

### Assessment

**Approved.** Clean, minimal, follows the Phase-1 primitive pattern. The `cn` + `twMerge` + `{...props}`-first ordering gets prop forwarding right, which is the most common bug in this category of component. Ship it.

I now have everything I need to review the commit. Here's my assessment.

---

## Review: commit `2c315c1` — Layout primitives (Card, Pill, StatBar, Spinner, Banner)

### What was reviewed
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/Card.tsx`
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/Pill.tsx`
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/StatBar.tsx`
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/Spinner.tsx`
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/Banner.tsx`
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/LayoutPrimitives.test.tsx`

### Strengths

- **Pattern consistency with Task 2.1/2.2** is strong. `cn(base, variants[tone], className)` in `Pill` and `Banner` mirrors `Button.tsx` exactly; `Card`, `StatBar`, `Spinner` use the same `cn(..., className)` tail that keeps the ergonomics uniform. `FormFields.tsx` uses the same shared-string convention.
- **`color-mix` arbitrary-value syntax is correct.** Tailwind requires underscores in place of spaces inside `bg-[...]`, and every occurrence does exactly that: `bg-[color-mix(in_srgb,var(--color-accent)_12%,transparent)]`. The commas are left as-is (legal in Tailwind arbitrary values), and `var(--color-*)` is not mangled.
- **One responsibility per component file.** Each primitive is a single small component that does one thing — `Pill` is tone mapping, `Banner` is a status strip, `StatBar` is a grid-of-stats, `Spinner` is a spinning glyph, `Card` is a bordered surface.
- **Test pinning matches repo discipline.** Tests assert on exact `text-[var(--color-accent)]` / `border-[var(--color-warn)]` class names rather than computed colors — a refactor that changes the Tailwind arbitrary values will trip the tests, which is the intent.
- **Design tokens sourced from `@theme` vars consistently** — no hard-coded hex values. Matches the project convention from Task 2.1.
- **Accessibility is considered**: `Spinner` has both `role="status"` and `aria-label`, which is the correct combination (AT announces the label on spinner appearance).

### Issues

#### Important (80–89)

**1. `role="note"` on Banner is a weak/inappropriate ARIA choice — confidence 85**
- File: `apps/web/src/components/ui/Banner.tsx:19`
- `role="note"` is an advisory/footnote role ("a section whose content is parenthetic or ancillary"). For a `warn`/`danger` banner, screen readers won't announce it on appearance and won't convey urgency. The standard pattern is:
  - `info` → `role="status"` (polite live region) or no role
  - `warn`/`danger` → `role="alert"` (assertive live region)
- Concrete fix: derive role from variant, e.g.
  ```tsx
  const roles: Record<Variant, "status" | "alert"> = {
    info: "status", warn: "alert", danger: "alert",
  };
  ...
  <div role={roles[variant]} ...>
  ```
  Or accept a `role` prop override. Either is consistent with the "tone → semantics" shape `Pill` already uses.

#### Minor, flagged below the 80 threshold for completeness (not gating)

- `Card.tsx` uses `rounded-[6px]` while `Button`, `Banner`, and `FormFields` all use `rounded-[5px]`, and `Pill` uses `rounded-[3px]`. `StatBar` also uses `rounded-[6px]`. Two radii (`5` vs `6`) across five new files is inconsistent — likely worth a design-token (`--radius-sm`, `--radius-md`) but not a violation of any explicit CLAUDE.md rule. Confidence ~55.
- `Pill` uses `bg-[var(--color-surface)]` for the `neutral` tone, but `Pill` is commonly rendered *inside* a `Card` whose background is already `bg-[var(--color-surface)]` — the neutral pill will be invisible against a card. Worth a visual check in Storybook/app context. Confidence ~60 without seeing the palette.
- The combined `LayoutPrimitives.test.tsx` file is appropriate here (shared vocabulary, small components, ships together) and consistent with `FormFields.test.tsx` which groups `Input/Textarea/Select/Field`. No issue.
- `Spinner` has no `size` prop; hard-coded `h-4 w-4`. Fine for now but will need revisiting when it's used alongside large headings. Not a current bug.

### Assessment

**Changes requested (minor)** — one real accessibility concern on `Banner`'s `role="note"`. Everything else (file layout, `cn` shape, color-mix syntax, test grouping, token usage, Spinner a11y) is clean and matches the prior primitive tasks. Once `Banner` announces correctly for `warn`/`danger`, this is approve-worthy.

content.text: Quality review flagged 3 Important issues (timer leak, click-race, silent clipboard rejection). Given Task 2.3's precedent, applying the fix now with a fake-timer test. Dispatching fix implementer.

## Report

**Status:** DONE

**Step 0 (pre-flight) verbatim:**
```
/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives
feat/web-redesign-ui-primitives
eab3699 feat(web): add CopyButton primitive
b8d9f5a fix(web): Banner announces warn/danger via role=alert
```

**Step 3 (test run) verbatim — final green run:**
```
 ✓ src/components/ui/CopyButton.test.tsx (5 tests) 162ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  5 passed (5)
```
5 passed as expected.

**Step 5 (git status) verbatim:**
```
 M apps/web/src/components/ui/CopyButton.test.tsx
 M apps/web/src/components/ui/CopyButton.tsx
```
Exact match to expected.

**Step 7 (git log) verbatim:**
```
57bf9d1 fix(web): CopyButton handles clipboard errors and timer cleanup
eab3699 feat(web): add CopyButton primitive
b8d9f5a fix(web): Banner announces warn/danger via role=alert
2c315c1 feat(web): add Card, Pill, StatBar, Spinner, Banner primitives
63f8a1d feat(web): add Input / Textarea / Select / Field primitives
66ad777 feat(web): add Button primitive with 4 variants
d45f1c3 feat(web): Linear-style redesign with navigable explorer and transaction history (#9)
```

**Concerns / deviations from the prescribed test code:**

The prescribed fake-timer tests as specified in the plan did not work in this environment and I had to adjust them (still only within `CopyButton.test.tsx`, no other files touched):

1. `Object.assign(navigator, { clipboard: { writeText } })` in `beforeEach` — JSDOM 25 has `navigator.clipboard` as a getter-only property after the first assignment, causing `TypeError: Cannot set property clipboard of #<Navigator> which has only a getter` on the 2nd+ test setup. Replaced with `Object.defineProperty(navigator, "clipboard", { value: { writeText }, configurable: true, writable: true })`.

2. `userEvent.setup({ advanceTimers: vi.advanceTimersByTime })` — user-event v14.5.2 still awaits real microtasks internally, so `user.click(...)` hung for 5s under fake timers (both fake-timer tests timed out). Switched those two tests to `fireEvent.click(...)` wrapped in `act(async () => { ... })`, which is synchronous and doesn't need timer advancement. Also scoped `vi.useFakeTimers({ toFake: ["setTimeout", "clearTimeout"] })` to only the specific timers the component uses, which keeps promise microtasks on real scheduling.

The component implementation (`CopyButton.tsx`) matches the plan-prescribed code byte-for-byte. All 3 bugs from review (unmount leak, click-race timer reset, silent clipboard rejection) are fixed and covered by pinned assertions. Typecheck passes. Commit `57bf9d1` landed on the feature branch.

input.prompt:
Quick verification of commit `57bf9d1` in the Orpheus web-redesign worktree. This is a follow-up fix for `CopyButton` that the previous review flagged (timer leak, click race, silent clipboard rejection).

## Where
- Worktree: `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives`
- Base SHA: `eab3699` (original CopyButton commit)
- Fix SHA: `57bf9d1` (the one under review)

## What should have happened

The fix should address 3 issues:
1. `setTimeout` cleanup on unmount via `useEffect` return + `useRef` for timer id
2. Clear previous timer on each click so quick re-clicks don't end the "Copied" state early
3. `try/catch` around `navigator.clipboard.writeText` so rejections don't silently break the UI

The implementer reported 5 tests pass, and they had to make two test-environment adjustments:
- `Object.defineProperty(navigator, "clipboard", ...)` instead of `Object.assign` because JSDOM 25 makes `navigator.clipboard` getter-only after first set.
- Used `fireEvent.click` + `act()` instead of `userEvent.click` under fake timers because user-event v14 awaits real microtasks that hang under fake timers.

## Your Job

Verify independently:

1. Run `cd /Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives && git diff eab3699..57bf9d1 -- apps/web/src/components/ui/CopyButton.tsx` and confirm:
   - `useRef<number | null>` added
   - `useEffect` cleanup added
   - `try { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(value); } catch { console.error(...); return; }` around the write
   - old timer cleared before new `setTimeout` set

2. Run `cd /Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives && cat apps/web/src/components/ui/CopyButton.test.tsx` and confirm there are 5 tests and the assertions match behavior (not just "timer ran"):

Quick verification of commit `57bf9d1` in the Orpheus web-redesign worktree. This is a follow-up fix for `CopyButton` that the previous review flagged (timer leak, click race, silent clipboard rejection).

- Base SHA: `eab3699` (original CopyButton commit)
- Fix SHA: `57bf9d1` (the one under review)

## What should have happened

The fix should address 3 issues:
1. `setTimeout` cleanup on unmount via `useEffect` return + `useRef` for timer id
2. Clear previous timer on each click so quick re-clicks don't end the "Copied" state early
3. `try/catch` around `navigator.clipboard.writeText` so rejections don't silently break the UI

The implementer reported 5 tests pass, and they had to make two test-environment adjustments:
- `Object.defineProperty(navigator, "clipboard", ...)` instead of `Object.assign` because JSDOM 25 makes `navigator.clipboard` getter-only after first set.
- Used `fireEvent.click` + `act()` instead of `userEvent.click` under fake timers because user-event v14 awaits real microtasks that hang under fake timers.

Review code quality for Task 2.5 of the Orpheus web-redesign.

## Where
- Worktree: `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives`
- Commit: `25007bb` — `feat(web): add WifReveal with auto-clear clipboard`
- Run `cd /Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives && git show 25007bb` for the diff.

## Context — this is security-sensitive

**Orpheus** is an offline Bitcoin wallet key recovery tool. `WifReveal` is the primary UI gate between the user and their raw private keys. A bug here leaks the key. Read the `CLAUDE.md` in the repo for the project's security posture.

- Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-web-redesign.md` lines 1703–1829
- **Design intent** (from the spec doc `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-web-redesign-design.md` §6):
  - Locked by default, `••••••••••` placeholder
  - **No hover-to-reveal**. **No auto-reveal on navigation.** Must require explicit click.
  - Reveal button starts a 20-second clipboard-auto-clear timer on Copy
  - `aria-label="Private key (hidden — activate reveal to show)"` on hidden state
- Phase 1 dependency: `apps/web/src/lib/clipboard.ts` exports `copyWithAutoClear(value, clearAfterMs)` — fires `navigator.clipboard.writeText("")` after delay to wipe the clipboard.

## What was implemented

- `apps/web/src/components/ui/WifReveal.tsx` — single component:
  - `useState<boolean>(false)` for `revealed`
  - Danger-tone bordered box (red border, subtle red-tint bg via `color-mix`)
  - Hidden state: `•` bullets in mono, with `aria-label="Private key (hidden — activate reveal to show)"`
  - Revealed state: raw `wif` in mono + Copy + Hide buttons
  - Copy calls `copyWithAutoClear(wif, 20_000)` — fire-and-forget, no `try/catch` or `await`
- `apps/web/src/components/ui/WifReveal.test.tsx` — 5 tests: default hidden, aria-label, reveal, copy, hide-again.

## Your Job

Focus only on commit `25007bb`. Report:
- Strengths
- Issues (Critical / Important only)
- Assessment (Approved / Changes requested)

Check in particular:
- **Security:** is the WIF ever leaked to the DOM before reveal? Is the bullet-count tied to key length (timing channel)?
- Does the Copy button's unhandled promise rejection risk claiming success while nothing was copied? This was the bug we just fixed in `CopyButton.tsx` — same class of issue.
- Is 20-second auto-clear invoked correctly (right unit, right argument order)?
- `break-all` + `flex-1` on the revealed span — does long WIF wrap reasonably vs. clipping?
- Keyboard/focus: does the reveal button have visible focus? Does toggling reveal steal focus or lose it?
- Any risk of the WIF persisting in React dev tools or stale fiber state after Hide?
- A11y: is the hidden-state `aria-label` on a `<span>` correctly picked up (spans aren't interactive landmarks)?

Skip pre-existing repo issues.

Keep concise.

# Code Review: commit `25007bb` — `WifReveal`

**Files reviewed:**
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/WifReveal.tsx`
- `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives/apps/web/src/components/ui/WifReveal.test.tsx`

**Context consulted:** `apps/web/src/lib/clipboard.ts`, `apps/web/src/components/ui/CopyButton.tsx` (the prior fix the reviewer flagged as precedent), `apps/web/src/components/ui/Button.tsx`.

---

## Strengths

- **Explicit click-to-reveal.** No `onMouseEnter` / `onFocus` reveal, no "reveal on mount" — meets the "no hover, no auto-reveal" spec.
- **DOM leakage is clean.** In the hidden branch the revealed `<span>` is never rendered (ternary picks one branch), so React does not emit the WIF into DOM text until the user clicks. The bullets are a hardcoded literal string, not derived from `wif.length` — no timing / length side-channel. Good.
- **No shadow copy.** `wif` is read straight from props; nothing like `useRef(wif)` or `useMemo(() => wif.slice(…))` that would let a stale copy outlive a Hide.
- **Auto-clear wiring.** `copyWithAutoClear(wif, 20_000)` passes value first, delay-in-ms second — matches the signature in `apps/web/src/lib/clipboard.ts` (`value: string, clearAfterMs: number`) and `20_000` is the right unit (ms, 20s) per spec §6.
- **Tests cover the state machine** (default hidden, reveal, hide-again, copy, aria-label).

---

## Issues

### Critical (90–100)

Review code quality for Task 2.5 of the Orpheus web-redesign.

## Where
- Worktree: `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives`
- Commit: `25007bb` — `feat(web): add WifReveal with auto-clear clipboard`
- Run `cd /Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives && git show 25007bb` for the diff.

## Context — this is security-sensitive

**Orpheus** is an offline Bitcoin wallet key recovery tool. `WifReveal` is the primary UI gate between the user and their raw private keys. A bug here leaks the key. Read the `CLAUDE.md` in the repo for the project's security posture.

- Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-web-redesign.md` lines 1703–1829
- **Design intent** (from the spec doc `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-web-redesign-design.md` §6):
  - Locked by default, `••••••••••` placeholder
  - **No hover-to-reveal**. **No auto-reveal on navigation.** Must require explicit click.
  - Reveal button starts a 20-second clipboard-auto-clear timer on Copy
  - `aria-label="Private key (hidden — activate reveal to show)"` on hidden state
- Phase 1 dependency: `apps/web/src/lib/clipboard.ts` exports `copyWithAutoClear(value, clearAfterMs)` — fires `navigator.clipboard.writeText("")` after delay to wipe the clipboard.

## What was implemented

- `apps/web/src/components/ui/WifReveal.tsx` — single component:
  - `useState<boolean>(false)` for `revealed`
  - Danger-tone bordered box (red border, subtle red-tint bg via `color-mix`)
  - Hidden state: `•` bullets in mono, with `aria-label="Private key (hidden — activate reveal to show)"`
  - Revealed state: raw `wif` in mono + Copy + Hide buttons
  - Copy calls `copyWithAutoClear(wif, 20_000)` — fire-and-forget, no `try/catch` or `await`
- `apps/web/src/components/ui/WifReveal.test.tsx` — 5 tests: default hidden, aria-label, reveal, copy, hide-again.

## Your Job

Focus only on commit `25007bb`. Report:
- Strengths
- Issues (Critical / Important only)
- Assessment (Approved / Changes requested)

Check in particular:
- **Security:** is the WIF ever leaked to the DOM before reveal? Is the bullet-count tied to key length (timing channel)?
- Does the Copy button's unhandled promise rejection risk claiming success while nothing was copied? This was the bug we just fixed in `CopyButton.tsx` — same class of issue.
- Is 20-second auto-clear invoked correctly (right unit, right argument order)?
- `break-all` + `flex-1` on the revealed span — does long WIF wrap reasonably vs. clipping?
- Keyboard/focus: does the reveal button have visible focus? Does toggling reveal steal focus or lose it?
- Any risk of the WIF persisting in React dev tools or stale fiber state after Hide?
- A11y: is the hidden-state `aria-label` on a `<span>` correctly picked up (spans aren't interactive landmarks)?

Skip pre-existing repo issues.

Keep concise.

Verify Task 2.6 (QRCode) matches plan spec.

## Where
- Worktree: `/Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/web-redesign-ui-primitives`

## Spec (Task 2.6, plan lines 1834–1938)

Create 2 new files:
- `apps/web/src/components/ui/QRCode.tsx` — SVG renderer using `qrcode-generator` library. JSX `<rect>` per dark module, **NO `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`**. Signature `{ value: string; size?: number }` (default 128). `qrcode(0, "M")`, `addData(value)`, `make()`, iterate `cells = qr.getModuleCount()`, collect `{ x, y }` for each `qr.isDark(y, x)` (note: isDark takes row, column — i.e. y first). Render `<svg width={size} height={size} viewBox=...>` with `role="img"`, `aria-label={"QR code for " + value}`, class `text-[var(--color-text)] bg-white p-1 rounded`, and JSX-render each rect with `fill="currentColor"`.
- `apps/web/src/components/ui/QRCode.test.tsx` — 2 tests.

## Your Job

Don't trust the implementer. Verify:

1. `git show --stat 977cb94` — exactly 2 new files.
2. `cat apps/web/src/components/ui/QRCode.tsx` — matches plan verbatim.
3. `cat apps/web/src/components/ui/QRCode.test.tsx` — matches plan verbatim.
4. **Security check:** `grep -n dangerouslySetInnerHTML apps/web/src/components/ui/QRCode.tsx` should return nothing. `grep` for `innerHTML` too.
5. The `isDark` argument order is `(y, x)` — row first.
6. The `aria-label` is the only place `value` appears as rendered text (since it's a user-supplied string and React will escape it there).

```bash
cat apps/web/src/components/ui/QRCode.tsx
cat apps/web/src/components/ui/QRCode.test.tsx
grep -n -E "dangerouslySetInnerHTML|innerHTML" apps/web/src/components/ui/QRCode.tsx || echo "no dangerous sinks"
```

## Report

- ✅ Spec compliant, or
- ❌ Issues: file:line

Keep short.

- Strengths
- Issues (Critical / Important)
- Assessment

- `bg-white` in Tailwind — does it preserve light/dark theme symmetry? The plan specifies white bg + currentColor fill.
